name: Mabel Mercer
abstract: Mabel Mercer (3 February 1900 – 20 April 1984) was an English-born cabaret singer who performed in the United States, Britain, and Europe with the greats in jazz and cabaret. She was a featured performer at Chez Bricktop in Paris, owned by the hostess Ada "Bricktop" Smith, and performed in such clubs as Le Ruban Bleu, Tony's, the RSVP, t…

concerts.label: Kool Jazz Festival: Friends of Alec Wilder

about_quotes.speaker: Alberta Hunter
about_quotes.evidence: We're the best of friends. Sure, Jimmy and Mabel and Brick and all of us, we get together. That's my group.

about_quotes.speaker: Sam Wooding
about_quotes.evidence: That's when Mabel Mercer and some people from England joined the show, you know. ... Yeah, she was with us and she stayed on with us

about_quotes.speaker: Big Nick Nicholas
about_quotes.evidence: Anybody that had a sound. Bessie Smith, Paul Robeson, Mahalia Jackson and Mabel Mercer and Billie Holiday.

about_quotes.speaker: Dorothy Donegan
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Did you ever meet Mabel Mercer? [INTERVIEWEE] Oh yeah. Who hasn’t?
about_quotes.doc_title: Dorothy Donegan — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Arthur Briggs
about_quotes.evidence: You've heard of Mabel Mercer? ... Well, Mabel Mercer was one of the singers.

about_quotes.speaker: Jacques Butler
about_quotes.evidence: That's the last time I saw her. She was singing that beautiful stuff
